quick_facts.label: Award and level
quick_facts.value: B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) is an integrated undergraduate law programme that combines business administration and legal studies.

quick_facts.label: Standard duration
quick_facts.value: The programme is commonly structured as five academic years over ten semesters; the exact academic calendar and course load are set by the university.

quick_facts.label: CLAT UG baseline for 2026
quick_facts.value: For CLAT 2026 UG, candidates needed 10+2/equivalent with at least 45% marks, or 40% for SC/ST/PwD candidates; there was no upper age limit. This is CLAT eligibility, not a universal admission rule for every B.B.A. LL.B. provider.

quick_facts.label: Professional-practice checkpoint
quick_facts.value: For an advocacy pathway, students should verify that the particular law course and institution are recognised or approved by the Bar Council of India (BCI). BCI states that candidates from non-recognised/non-approved institutions are ineligible for AIBE.

quick_facts.label: Fees are institution-specific
quick_facts.value: As dated 2026 context, Mahindra University lists annual academic fees of ₹4,00,000, hostel charges of ₹1,70,000, and a refundable ₹20,000 caution deposit for its B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) programme; it also states that fees may be revised annually. This is an example, not an India-wide fee benchmark.

sections.heading: Eligibility: Class 12 qualification, with rules varying by admission route
paragraphs: The usual entry point is completion of Class 12 (10+2) or an equivalent qualification. For the CLAT 2026 UG route, the Consortium of NLUs required 45% marks for general candidates and 40% for SC/ST/PwD candidates; candidates taking the qualifying examination in March or April 2026 could apply provisionally, subject to producing proof of passing at admission.

Universities may impose different thresholds and category rules. For example, Mahatma Gandhi University’s 2026–27 B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) notification specifies 45% aggregate for its general seats, 42% for OBC candidates, and 40% for SC/ST and persons with disability. Applicants should therefore treat the target university’s current prospectus and admission notice as controlling.

Where the intended outcome includes enrolment and legal practice, choose a BCI-recognised university or BCI-approved college affiliated with a BCI-recognised university. BCI’s AIBE guidance makes this recognition status an eligibility condition for the examination.
bullets: CLAT 2026 UG: 10+2/equivalent; 45% minimum, reduced to 40% for SC/ST/PwD; no upper age limit.
Institutional rules can be stricter or use different reservation/category thresholds.
Before accepting an offer, verify the programme’s current BCI recognition/approval status if practice as an advocate is a goal.

sections.heading: Duration and structure
paragraphs: B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) is normally delivered as a full-time five-year integrated programme. Nirma University describes its B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) as five years spread across ten semesters, and Mahindra University likewise describes a five-year, ten-semester format.

The integrated structure means that business and management study is undertaken alongside the law curriculum rather than through two separately completed undergraduate degrees. Individual institutions determine their credit rules, examinations, internship requirements, and honours papers.

sections.heading: Curriculum: business foundations plus core and applied law
paragraphs: The curriculum is interdisciplinary: early study can combine management, accounting, economics, communication, legal methods, torts, and contract law. Nirma University’s published structure, for example, lists Legal Methods, Law of Torts, economics, financial accounting, and business organisation and management in its first semester.

The legal component typically progresses through constitutional law, criminal law, family law, jurisprudence, company law, civil procedure, evidence, intellectual-property law, taxation, labour law, environmental law, public and private international law, and electives. The management component may include marketing, finance, strategy, operations, organisational behaviour, and business research.

Applied legal learning is a significant part of published curricula. The University of Mumbai’s B.B.A.-LL.B. (Hons.) syllabus includes clinical legal education, drafting, moot-court exercise, and internship; its published curriculum should be read as an institutional example rather than a uniform national syllabus.
bullets: Business and management: accounting, economics, finance, marketing, organisational and strategic management.
Law: contracts, torts, constitutional and criminal law, company law, procedure, evidence, taxation, labour law, intellectual property, and electives.
Practice-oriented work may include clinical courses, drafting, ADR, moots, and internships.

sections.heading: Admission: CLAT is an important route, but not the only one
paragraphs: CLAT is the common admission test for five-year integrated LL.B. admissions at participating universities in the Consortium system. The Consortium’s 2026 FAQ states that CLAT 2026 covered admissions to five-year integrated LL.B. and LL.M. programmes at participating universities for the 2026–27 academic year.

A B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.) applicant must still check whether the target institution offers this specific stream through CLAT and whether it has additional requirements. Some institutions use multiple routes: Mahindra University’s 2026 B.B.A. LL.B. page accepted a valid CLAT score, LSAT score, or its own MULET entrance test, along with its academic eligibility requirement.

Application dates, seat matrices, counselling processes, category reservations, and required documents change by institution and admission year. Use the current official notice for the intended university rather than relying on a prior year’s cutoff or deadline.
bullets: Check that the target institution actually offers B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.), not only another integrated law stream.
Confirm the accepted test route: CLAT, an institutional test, or another stated examination.
Read the current prospectus for category rules, counselling, documents, and payment deadlines.

sections.heading: Costs: compare current official fee schedules, not generic averages
paragraphs: There is no standard national fee for B.B.A. LL.B. (Hons.). Tuition, hostel, mess, deposits, examination charges, and annual escalation policies vary substantially between public, private, residential, and non-residential providers.

As a dated illustration for 2026 admissions, Mahindra University lists ₹4,00,000 annual academic fees, ₹1,70,000 annual hostel charges, and a refundable ₹20,000 caution deposit for the programme. The university explicitly notes that fees are subject to annual revision and that hostel residence is not mandatory for these law programmes.

Build a five-year budget from the target institution’s current fee sheet and separate refundable deposits from recurring academic, accommodation, and meal costs. Also check whether scholarships, financial aid, or category-based concessions apply under that institution’s current rules.
bullets: Use the official fee schedule for the relevant admission year.
Separate tuition, accommodation, meals, examinations, one-time charges, and refundable deposits.
Treat published amounts as dated and subject to revision.

sections.heading: Next options after graduation
paragraphs: LL.M. is a direct postgraduate law option. For the CLAT 2026 PG route, the Consortium required an LL.B. degree or equivalent with at least 50% marks, reduced to 45% for SC/ST/PwD candidates; individual LL.M. programmes may have their own additional conditions.

For court practice, the relevant pathway is enrolment with a State Bar Council followed by the All India Bar Examination (AIBE), subject to the current BCI rules. BCI describes AIBE as a qualifying examination for practising advocates and states that successful candidates receive a certificate of practice; its eligibility guidance also requires study at a BCI-recognised university or BCI-approved affiliated college.

A B.B.A. LL.B. graduate is also a graduate for purposes of the ICSI CS Executive route. ICSI’s current student-services FAQ states that graduates and postgraduates can obtain direct admission to the CS Executive Programme and are listed among categories exempt from CSEET.

Other progression routes in the node—such as an MBA, state judicial-services examinations, civil-services recruitment, JAG entry, PSU legal-officer vacancies, and corporate counsel roles—have separate, time-sensitive eligibility and recruitment rules. Applicants should rely on the current notification of the relevant university, state commission, armed service, PSU, or employer.
bullets: LL.M.: check the specific university’s eligibility; CLAT PG is one route for participating institutions.
Advocacy: verify BCI recognition/approval, State Bar Council enrolment requirements, and current AIBE rules.
CS Executive: ICSI identifies graduates as eligible for direct registration, with the stated CSEET exemption framework.
Employment and competitive-exam paths require checking the latest recruiting authority’s notification.
